a: Returns a formatted address from an address property.
A view controller presenting an interface to create a contact.
The `ABNewPersonViewControllerDelegate` protocol declares the interface that [ABNewPersonViewController](/documentation/addressbookui/abnewpersonviewcontroller) delegates must implement.

The `ABPeoplePickerNavigationController` class (whose instances are known as **people-picker navigation controllers**) implements a view controller that manages a set of views that allow the user to select a contact or one of its contact-information items from an address book.

The `ABPeoplePickerNavigationControllerDelegate` protocol describes the interface [ABPeoplePickerNavigationController](/documentation/addressbookui/abpeoplepickernavigationcontroller) delegates must adopt to respond to people-picker user events.
The person’s birthday. Value is an [NSDateComponents](/documentation/Foundation/NSDateComponents) object.
The person’s date. Value is an array of labeled values with [NSDateComponents](/documentation/Foundation/NSDateComponents) values.
The person’s department name. Value is an [NSString](/documentation/Foundation/NSString) object.
The person’s email address. Value is an array of labeled values with [NSString](/documentation/Foundation/NSString) values.
The person’s family name. Value is an [NSString](/documentation/Foundation/NSString) object.
The person’s given name. Value is an [NSString](/documentation/Foundation/NSString) object.
The name prefix. Value is an array of labeled values with instant message address values. An instant message address has a “username” property and a “service” property.
The person’s job title. Value is an [NSString](/documentation/Foundation/NSString) object.
The person’s middle name. Value is an [NSString](/documentation/Foundation/NSString) object.
The name prefix. Value is an [NSString](/documentation/Foundation/NSString) object.
The name suffix. Value is an [NSString](/documentation/Foundation/NSString) object.
The person’s nickname. Value is an [NSString](/documentation/Foundation/NSString) object.
The note for the person. Value is an [NSString](/documentation/Foundation/NSString) object.
The person’s organization name. Value is an [NSString](/documentation/Foundation/NSString) object.
The person’s phone numbers. Value is an array of labeled values with phone number values.
The person’s phonetic family name. Value is an [NSString](/documentation/Foundation/NSString) object.
The person’s phonetic given name. Value is an [NSString](/documentation/Foundation/NSString) object.
The person’s phonetic middle name. Value is an [NSString](/documentation/Foundation/NSString) object.
The person’s postal addresses.
The person’s previous family name. Value is an [NSString](/documentation/Foundation/NSString) object.
The person’s related names. Value is an array of labeled values with [NSString](/documentation/Foundation/NSString) values.
The person’s social profiles. Value is an array of labeled values with social profile values. A social profile has a “username” property and a “service” property.
The person’s URL addresses. Value is an array of labeled values with [NSString](/documentation/Foundation/NSString) values.
The `ABPersonViewController` class (whose instances are known as **person view controllers**) implements the view used to display a person record (`ABPersonRef`).
The `ABPersonViewControllerDelegate` protocol declares the interface that must be implemented by [ABPersonViewController](/documentation/addressbookui/abpersonviewcontroller) delegates.

The `ABUnknownPersonViewController` class (whose instances are known as **unknown-person view controllers**) implements a view controller used to create a person record from a set of person properties.
The methods you use to respond to events in an unknown person view controller.
These constants can be used in predicates for selecting people or properties. A labeled value is an object with a “label” property and a “value” property.
Access users’ contacts and display them in a graphical interface.
Optional; the address book from which to obtain the list of contacts.
Optional. The address book to which the new contact is added.
Optional. The address book database that the person record is added to.
Optional. The address book from which to obtain the contact to display.
Specifies whether buttons appear to let the user perform actions such as sharing the contact, initiating a FaceTime call, or sending a text message.
Specifies whether the to display buttons for actions such as sending a text message or initiating a FaceTime call.
Specifies whether the user can add the properties displayed by the unknown-person view controller to the address book database, either as a new contact or by adding them to an existing contact.
Specifies whether the user can edit the person’s information.
Provides a value that is displayed instead of the first and last name.
Optional. Specifies the person properties that the new-person view controller pre-fills in its views.
Specifies a person record whose properties are displayed by the view controller.
The person displayed by the person view.
The properties (such as name or telephone number) the picker displays when it shows a person.
Identifies the set of properties (such as name or telephone number) of [displayedPerson](/documentation/addressbookui/abpersonviewcontroller/displayedperson) the receiver displays.
Text displayed below [alternateName](/documentation/addressbookui/abunknownpersonviewcontroller/alternatename).
Sent when the user taps Save or Cancel. If the user tapped Save, the current address book has been saved to the Address Book database.
The delegate of a new-person view controller.
Optional. Specifies the group to which to add the new contact on save.
The people-picker navigation controller delegate.
Called after a person has been selected by the user.
Called after a property has been selected by the user.
Sent when the user selects a contact.
Sent when the user selects one of a person’s properties.
Sent when the user taps Cancel.
Sent when the user selects a property value of the person displayed in a person view controller.
The person-view controller delegate.
Optionally determines if a person can be selected.
Optionally determines if a selected person should be returned to the app or displayed.
Optionally determines if a selected property should be returned to the app or if the default action for the property should be performed
Specifies whether to highlight a particular property of the displayed person.
Indicates whether the person view should display data from person records that are linked with the person record being displayed.
Sent when the user finishes creating a contact or adding the displayed person properties to an existing contact.
Sent when the user selects a property value of the person displayed in a person view controller.
The unknown-person view controller delegate.
